entries.mem0ai/mem0: Mem0 is a universal memory layer shipped as an official Hermes Agent memory provider — one `hermes memory setup` command configures it with an API key from app.mem0.ai. The LLM automatically gains three tools (mem0_profile, mem0_search with reranking, mem0_conclude), while server-side extraction and background-thread calls protected by a circuit breaker keep conversation latency at zero. Mem0 is Apache-2.0, so the same provider also runs fully self-hosted via the OSS Python SDK or a Docker Compose stack (Postgres + Qdrant) for teams that need to keep memory on their own infrastructure.
entries.garrytan/gbrain: A self-wiring knowledge graph that automates the ingestion of emails, meetings, and voice calls without requiring LLM calls for entity linking. It is a powerful choice for users seeking deep integration via MCP servers for tools like Claude Code and Cursor.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: This system implements biomimetic data structures to facilitate long-term agent learning through dedicated retain, recall, and reflect workflows. It offers broad compatibility by integrating with OpenAI, Anthropic, and Ollama.
entries.greyhaven-ai/autocontext: A recursive context harness that utilizes a multi-agent loop of specialized roles, such as analysts and architects, to accumulate validated knowledge. It is designed to improve performance on complex, repeated tasks through verifier-driven checkpoints.
entries.stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm: A lossless context engine that uses a hierarchical DAG structure and immutable SQLite storage to ensure no conversation history is ever deleted. It features a three-level escalation mechanism to guarantee context convergence during complex interactions.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: This backend provides a private, self-hosted solution for cross-session memory persistence. It is optimized for local control, supporting any OpenAI-compatible provider and featuring automated setup via Docker, PostgreSQL, and Redis.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: A local-first, on-device memory engine that utilizes a unified SQLite vault to maintain persistence across different agents. It employs a hybrid retrieval strategy combining vector search, BM25, and graph traversal for robust context access.
entries.plur-ai/plur: A shared memory layer designed for multi-agent systems that utilizes an open YAML engram format for local storage. It enables zero-cost retrieval through hybrid search and automates memory injection for Hermes and MCP tools.
entries.keepnotes-ai/keep: This reflective memory system indexes codebases and media using automatic summarization and OCR. It uses edge tags to build bidirectional relationships between notes, accessible via MCP and CLI interfaces.
entries.AxDSan/mnemosyne: A high-performance, zero-dependency memory system providing sub-millisecond latency through SQLite-native vector search. It organizes information into a three-tier BEAM architecture consisting of working, episodic, and scratchpad memory.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: An anticipatory memory system that prefetches context by monitoring agent session logs. It is a local-first solution using SQLite-vec and GGUF models, featuring an MCP-native server for seamless integration with coding agents.

entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: This repository provides 754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to industry standards like MITRE ATT&CK and NIST CSF 2.0. It enables Hermes to operate across 26 security domains, including malware analysis and cloud security, using the agentskills.io integration standard.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: This tool audits and rewrites content to eliminate detectable AI patterns using a tiered 109-entry word replacement table. It features a two-pass audit system compatible with Hermes Agent to ensure natural-sounding, human-like text generation.
entries.wondelai/skills: A cross-platform library that provides modular skills for architecture, UX design, and product strategy via a dedicated CLI. It allows Hermes to quickly adopt professional frameworks through an npx-based marketplace compatible with the agentskills.io standard.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: This project implements the Agent Skills open specification for Pydantic AI with a focus on type safety and token efficiency. It uses progressive disclosure of metadata to reduce context usage while supporting both filesystem and programmatic skill definitions.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: This skill enables Hermes to generate six types of professional diagrams, including UML and ML architectures, directly from natural language. It features a five-round self-check safety valve and coordinate snapping to ensure precise, grid-aligned visual outputs.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: Designed for blockchain interoperability, this skill set allows agents to perform CCIP cross-chain sends and monitor multi-chain data feeds. It follows the agentskills.io spec to provide Hermes with standardized smart contract interaction and local testing capabilities.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: This literate programming tool allows Hermes to weave together prose, diagrams, and source code into unified .lit.md files. It includes a tangler to extract runnable code and automatically syncs edits between documentation and source files.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: Icarus provides Hermes with cross-instance shared memory and a pipeline for training its own replacement models. It extracts decision-quality data from active workflows into markdown-based fabric directories for fine-tuning and evaluation.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: This registry offers an OpenAPI-driven SDK for managing the lifecycle of reusable AI skills with built-in monetization via Stripe and TON. It simplifies Hermes' expansion by handling automated dependency resolution and local skill installation.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: These official skills provide standardized prompting guidelines and API integration patterns for the FLUX.1 and FLUX.2 image generation models. They enable Hermes to utilize precise hex colors and structured JSON prompting for high-fidelity visual tasks.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: A meta-skill plugin that monitors active workflows to automatically detect and propose new, reusable skills. It generates both Python scaffolds and documentation, allowing Hermes to autonomously expand its own capabilities via slash commands.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: Maestro is an orchestration harness that manages long-running missions through structured milestones and validation assertions. It persists agent state locally, allowing Hermes to hand off complex tasks to other models using generated markdown briefs.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: This skill facilitates the autonomous execution of multi-step tasks with real-time progress tracking and decision logging. It includes built-in rollback procedures and checkpoints to ensure Hermes can safely recover from errors during long-running operations.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: This progressive Model Context Protocol client uses three-layer lazy loading to significantly reduce token consumption. It maintains persistent server connections via a socket-based daemon and supports hot-reloading for seamless configuration updates.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: This forge converts existing code repositories and developer documentation into version-pinned, agentskills.io-compliant instructions. It provides verifiable audit trails with file-and-line level provenance to ensure the reliability of generated agent skills.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: A community-driven hub for browsing and installing skills that features automated security scanning for prompt injection and data exfiltration. It supports micropayment protocols for premium content and an agent-to-agent feedback system based on proof-of-use.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: This C# implementation of the agentskills.io specification brings MCP integration to the .NET ecosystem. It supports automatic skill discovery and utilizes progressive disclosure to save tokens when Hermes interacts with .NET-based tools.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: This project provides reproducible Nix packages for Hermes and other AI coding agents, ensuring consistent environments across different machines. It features automated daily updates and supports direct execution via Nix flakes, allowing users to run the latest version of Hermes without a traditional installation.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: Focused on high-security environments, this project implements hardware-enforced sandboxing using NVIDIA OpenShell and kernel-level syscall blocking. It includes a library of over 40 integrated tools and offers pre-configured security policies that range from strict inference to permissive system access.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: This repository offers a NixOS module for declarative management of Hermes configurations, documents, and systemd services. It prioritizes security through sops-nix integration for secrets handling and supports a hybrid skill model that combines Nix-managed tools with interactive CLI installs.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: Designed for Windows users, this zero-install desktop agent provides a GUI and over 100 built-in tools without requiring Docker. It supports local model execution via LM Studio and includes a dynamic tool maker for building custom automation pipelines and workflows.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: This project provides a streamlined Docker-based sandbox that includes both the official Hermes installation and mini-swe-agent. It supports persistent state through mounted directories and allows users to target specific Hermes versions using build arguments.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: Targeting production environments on Ubuntu and Debian, this deployment configures the Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service. It is optimized for headless operation and enables the execution of autonomous, recurring AI cron jobs through Nous Portal integration.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: This deployment option uses Docker Compose to provide a web-accessible Hermes agent via a ttyd terminal interface. It is designed to integrate directly with Portainer as a Git Repository stack and utilizes GitHub Actions for automated image builds.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: This self-hosted orchestration platform manages multi-agent workflows across frameworks like CrewAI and AutoGen using a zero-dependency SQLite backend. It is essential for Hermes users who require cost monitoring and a human-in-the-loop 'Aegis' system for manual task sign-off.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: Swarmclaw provides a self-hosted runtime for building autonomous agent swarms with native support for Hermes and 22 other model providers. It stands out by offering MCP-native skills, persistent memory, and scheduled heartbeats to maintain long-running agent delegations.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: This project implements a meta-optimization harness that uses an automated outer-loop search to refine the code used for evaluating Hermes. It orchestrates TBLite and TB2 benchmarks, tracking performance frontiers through structured candidate mutation and comparison.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: Gladiator creates a competitive arena where two autonomous AI companies, powered by Hermes Agent and Paperclip, vie for GitHub stars. The framework orchestrates nine specialized agents and visualizes their real-time tasks, code generation, and scoreboard metrics on a live dashboard.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: This framework coordinates a massive fleet of 17 specialized agents across distinct development phases from research to infrastructure. It ensures high-fidelity outputs by enforcing mandatory quality gates for code reviews and testing across multiple LLM backends.
entries.supermodeltools/bigiron: Bigiron automates an eight-phase software development lifecycle by providing Hermes with structural code graphs via the Supermodel MCP. It differentiates itself by using graph-diff phase gates to enforce architectural integrity throughout the autonomous coding process.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: This delegation tool routes tasks between Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code while preventing context pollution in the parent agent. It prioritizes operational safety by implementing isolated workflows with configurable timeouts and strict output character limits.

entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: This Rust-based TUI tracks token consumption and costs across multiple AI coding agents, including Hermes and Claude Code. It provides developers with local data visualization to monitor usage patterns and expenses across various model providers.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: This customization tool allows users to personalize the Hermes terminal interface through YAML-based themes, ASCII art, and custom spinner verbs. It supports partial skins that inherit from default themes and includes a variety of presets ranging from retro DOS to sci-fi aesthetics.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: This project ports the Hermes Agent brain and 46 core skills directly into the Claude Code Channel for native execution without external processes or OAuth. It specifically enhances the environment with MLOps tools for vLLM, GGUF inference, and reinforcement learning training.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: Vessel is an AI-native browser designed for autonomous agent control, offering a human-visible UI for auditing agent actions in real-time. It exposes structured page schemas via MCP and maintains persistent sessions with annotated checkpoints for supervised browsing tasks.
entries.Abruptive/Ankh.md: Ankh.md is a framework for creating scoped Hermes Agents that operate with per-folder configurations and project-local memories. It enables seamless switching between global and local agents, allowing for specialized tools and instructions tailored to specific directories.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: This static linter improves agent performance by detecting structural flaws in system prompts and tool descriptions using seven deterministic pattern detectors. It provides concrete fix suggestions based on HERM v1.1 scoring without requiring API keys or LLM overhead.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: This migration utility streamlines the transition from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent by mapping personas, memories, and complex model configurations to the Hermes YAML format. It automates the transfer of workspace files and credentials from local environment files to ensure a smooth setup.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: This deployment script uses Docker to launch a complete Hermes Agent stack featuring LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant for local GPU inference. It comes pre-configured with 29 community plugins for memory and social integration, focused on achieving a zero-cost setup using free models.

entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: A zero-build web interface that provides full feature parity with the Hermes Agent CLI. It utilizes a three-panel layout to streamline chat, file browsing, and session management across desktop and mobile browsers.
entries.EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui: A comprehensive dashboard focused on multi-platform connectivity and operational oversight. It features unified configuration for channels like Telegram and Discord, alongside real-time usage analytics for token consumption and costs.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: A security-hardened native web workspace featuring execution approval prompts and auth middleware. It offers deep orchestration tools, including a dedicated skills manager and a comprehensive memory browser.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: A self-hosted gateway designed for managing multiple agent profiles from a single dashboard. It combines a browser-based terminal and system metrics with detailed token analytics categorized by model and platform.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: A native macOS application that connects directly via SSH without requiring a remote helper service. It provides a professional multi-tabbed terminal with live theme controls for managing sessions and cron jobs.
entries.clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp: A mobile-first React SPA specifically designed as a Telegram Mini App. It enables background agent spawning with live output monitoring and integrated system health gauges for hardware resources.
entries.Web3CZ/Web3Hermes: A localized Chinese version of the Hermes WebUI tailored for accessibility within the Chinese community. It includes a workspace file browser for direct editing and tool call visualization within session management.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: A desktop companion app that simplifies the onboarding process through guided installation and dependency resolution. It supports a wide array of LLM providers and local endpoints alongside integrated messaging gateways.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: A specialized monitoring dashboard focused on process oversight and configuration. It features FTS5 search for conversation history inspection and real-time tracking of cost distribution.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: A self-hosted workspace that integrates a marketplace for MCP servers, plugins, and skills.sh, utilizing isolated profile management to separate sessions, memory, and API keys.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: A glassmorphic React 18 interface built on a single-file architecture. It features a multi-tab terminal that supports simultaneous Shell, Hermes, and Claude Code workflows.
